description: You are a Medical Geneticist designing diagnostic primers for beta-thalassemia. Using the Human Beta-Globin (HBB) gene sequence at ~/UGENE_Data/thalassemia_diagnostics/hbb_genomic.gb, identify and annotate the two introns that bridge the gaps between the three existing exon annotations. Export the updated GenBank file containing the new 'intron' annotations to ~/UGENE_Data/thalassemia_diagnostics/results/hbb_annotated_with_introns.gb. Extract the raw nucleotide sequences for Intron 1 and Intron 2 to ~/UGENE_Data/thalassemia_diagnostics/results/intron1.fasta and ~/UGENE_Data/thalassemia_diagnostics/results/intron2.fasta respectively. Finally, compile a sequence statistics report at ~/UGENE_Data/thalassemia_diagnostics/results/intron_statistics.txt for both introns, including their start and end coordinates, exact sequence length in base pairs, and GC content percentage (to at least 1 decimal place).

metadata.data_source: Real HBB sequence region (Human hemoglobin subunit beta, based on V00497.1) with true exon coordinates.
